body: Indonesia will keep subsidized fuel prices unchanged through 2026 as energy supply remains secure and oil price pressures stay manageable.

title: JCI Gains 0.32% on Global Risk Rally, S&P Keeps Indonesia at BBB Stable
body: JCI rose 0.32% at the open, tracking ceasefire optimism and steady BBB rating, despite persistent geopolitical and currency risks.

title: Gasoline Motorbike Ban Proposal Threatens Indonesia’s Two-Wheeler Duopoly
body: Indonesia sold 6.58 million new motorcycles in 2025 alone, making it one of the largest motorcycle markets globally.

title: Rupiah Strengthens Slightly to Rp 17,138 on Cooling US-Iran Tensions
body: Rupiah closed at Rp 17,138, gaining on easing US-Iran tensions and stronger external debt growth despite oil supply concerns.

title: JCI Pauses Rally, Edges Down as Investors Await Clarity on US–Iran Talks
body: JCI slips 0.03% to 7,621 as profit-taking and US–Iran uncertainty offset strong regional gains and easing oil price concerns.

title: Retail Sector Flags Cost Pressures, Urges Import Policy Relaxation
body: Retailers warn rising input costs are hitting prices and urge faster import permits to secure supply and curb domestic price pressures.

title: US Roadshow ‘Not a Game Changer’ Without Policy Credibility, Indef Says
body: Indonesia’s US roadshow may lift short-term sentiment, but Indef says policy credibility is key for lasting investor confidence.

title: Indonesia Eyes Fertilizer Exports to India on 1.5 Million-Ton Surplus
body: Indonesia eyes urea exports to India as a 1.5 million-ton surplus meets soaring global prices driven by supply disruptions.

title: Purbaya Says World Bank May Have Miscalculated Indonesia Growth Outlook
body: Indonesia receives positive feedback on fiscal strategy as Purbaya addresses World Bank’s growth outlook and highlights strong fundamentals.

title: Jakarta Stocks Rally Early on Ceasefire Buzz, Tracks Wall Street Highs
body: JCI climbed 0.52% at the open, riding a global rally as investors bet the US-Iran conflict is nearing a ceasefire.

title: Norway Reaps Boon from Oil, Gas Price Surge
body: Statistics Norway says export revenues in March soared to nearly 200 billion kroner ($21 billion) last month, up more than 28% yoy.

title: Rupiah Stays Under Pressure, Slips to Rp 17,143 on Middle East Tensions
body: Rupiah weakens to Rp 17,143 as Iran blockade fuels oil supply fears, raising inflation risks and reinforcing a higher-for-longer Fed outlook

title: Palm Oil Surface Water Tax Feared to Backfire on B50 Biodiesel
body: Indonesia is planning to expand the biodiesel program in July, but the proposed surface water tax can set a challenge to this policy.

title: Public Debt Surge Lifts Indonesia’s External Debt to $437.9B
body: Indonesia’s external debt rose to $437.9B in Feb as BI inflows boosted public debt, while private borrowing continued to contract.

title: Profit-Taking Drags JCI Down 0.68% to 7,623 on Wednesday
body: JCI fell 0.68% to 7,623 as profit-taking offset improving global sentiment on easing Middle East tensions and steady domestic policy outlook

title: BYD, VinFast Hiring Boosts Jobs in West Java
body: BYD and VinFast ramp up hiring in West Java, with nearly half of workers local as Indonesia accelerates its electric vehicle ambitions.

title: IMF Sees Global Growth Down to 3.1%, Indonesia to 5%
body: Global growth is seen at 3.1% in 2026 as war-driven energy shocks raise inflation and pressure Asian economies, including Indonesia.

title: IMF: New Oil Shock Rivals 1970s Crisis, But Policy Mistakes Could Decide Outcome
body: An oil shock comparable to the 1970s could derail global growth, though stronger central banks may help avoid prolonged stagflation.

title: Apindo Warns Production Could Stall Next Month on Supply Shortages
body: Apindo warns of supply disruptions and rising costs as Middle East tensions hit raw materials, raising risks to production stability

title: JCI Gains 0.98% as Diplomacy Lifts Markets, Indonesia Eyes Russian Crude
body: JCI rises to 7,750 at open as US–Iran diplomacy lifts global sentiment, oil eases, and Indonesia explores cheaper Russian crude imports.
